title: Internet Issues
cardText: Troubleshoot Wi-Fi, Ethernet, router, and device connection problems.
Check local connection issues before restarting network equipment.
Escalate possible ISP faults when basic checks do not resolve the issue.
text: Work through basic checks for a home or small office internet connection.
Escalate when the connection still fails.

checkConnection.title: Check Wi-Fi/Ethernet connection
body: Confirm the device is connected to the correct Wi-Fi network or that the Ethernet cable is firmly plugged in.
Reconnect if needed, then try loading a website again.

restartRouter.title: Restart modem/router
body: Wait 30 seconds, then turn it back on.
Wait for the status lights to settle before testing the connection again.

testAnotherDevice.title: Test another device
body: Try connecting to the internet from another phone, laptop, or tablet on the same network.
This helps confirm whether the issue is one device or the whole connection.

checkIspOutage.title: Check ISP outage
body: Check the internet provider's outage page or service status line.
If there is an outage, wait for the provider to restore service.

contactIsp.title: Escalate/contact ISP
body: Contact the internet provider with the troubleshooting already completed.
Ask them to test the line and confirm whether a technician visit is needed.
